seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: keep the min-cost connectivity goal but (1) restrict the output to a per-inverter port-capped connected spanning network (caps in {2,3,4}) and (2) generalize the objective from pure Euclidean length to L1 trench length PLUS per-inverter port-termination fees w_i*deg(i). This couples the NP-hard degree-constrained spanning-network problem with a node-degree-weighted cost, so a good layout must simultaneously stay short and keep expensive combiners off busy hubs.
title: Solar Farm DC Collection Grid: Port-Capped Trench Cabling
strategies: chain baseline: link inverters in input order into one path (always feasible, matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
nearest-neighbour path: repeatedly hop to the closest unvisited inverter (Manhattan), a short degree-2 chain that ignores termination fees

fee-aware degree-constrained Kruskal: add candidate short links whose endpoints still have a free port, weighting each link by trench(a,b)+w_a+w_b so expensive combiners are pushed to be leaves; repair leftover components via spare-capacity nodes
local search: start from a degree-constrained tree and apply cost-reducing edge swaps / branch reroutes (moving degree off high-fee inverters) that respect the port caps
